revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23986660476|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Hōtoku-Ninomiya-Schrein (Odawara)]][[en:Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine]][[ja:報徳二宮神社 (小田原市)]]
{{Short description|Shrine in Odawara, Kanagawa}} {{more citations needed|date=February 2018}}
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine | image = File:161223 Hotoku-Ninomiya-jinja Odawara Japan02n.jpg | caption = Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.25|N|139.15277778|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity ={{ill|Ninomiya Sontoku|qid=Q968964|1=Ninomiya Sontoku}} | founded_by = | established = | date_destroyed = | location = | website = | architecture_style ={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} | festival = | leadership = |Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine |location= Odawara City, Kanagawa Prefecture, Jōnai 8-10 |Rank = Prefectural shrine, Beppyo shrine |established = {{ill|1894|en|1894|qid=Q7843|lt=1894|1=1894}} (Meiji 27) |main hall = [[Shinmei-zukuri]] |alternative name = |festival = {{ill|April 15|en|April 15|qid=Q2497|lt=April 15|1=April 15}} | location = {{ill|Odawara|qid=Q267258|1=Odawara}} }} '''Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine''' (ほうとくにのみやじんじゃ) is a shrine located in [[Odawara City]], {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, dedicated to [[Ninomiya Sontoku]]. The shrine was formerly a [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|Prefectural shrine]] and is currently a [[Beppyo Shrine]] of the [[Association of Shinto Shrines]]. ''''Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine''' (報徳二宮神社) is a Japanese Shinto shrine dedicated to [[Ninomiya Sontoku]] (二宮尊徳) and is located in the City of [[Odawara, Kanagawa|Odawara]] in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=報徳二宮神社 神奈川県小田原城内 |url=http://www.ninomiya.or.jp |access-date=2018-01-29 |website=www.ninomiya.or.jp |language=ja}}</ref>
== History == Ninomiya Sontoku established the [[Hōtoku Society]] and conducted relief and educational activities for rural villages. After Sontoku's death in {{ill|1856|en|1856|qid=Q7676|lt=1856|1=1856}} (Ansei 3), the Hōtoku Society continued its activities, primarily in the Kanto and Tokai regions. In {{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}} (Meiji 24), Sontoku was posthumously awarded the Rank of [[Jūshi-i]] (Senior Fourth Rank), prompting members of the Hōtoku Society to advocate for the establishment of a shrine in his honor. On April 15, {{ill|1894|en|1894|qid=Q7843|lt=1894|1=1894}} (Meiji 27), the shrine was enshrined within the grounds of {{ill|Odawara Castle|en|Odawara Castle|qid=Q1013437|lt=Odawara Castle|1=Odawara Castle}}, Sontoku's birthplace.
== Facilities == * main hall * Shrine Office * Hōtoku Hall - Includes the restaurant Juma and Café Ginger * Hōtoku Museum - Located in front of the torii gate, this facility houses and preserves materials related to Ninomiya Sontoku and the Hōtoku Society * Kinjiro Café - An open café located in the shrine garden <gallery> File:161223 Hotoku-Ninomiya-jinja Odawara Japan03n.jpg|main hall File:Statue of Ninomiya Sontoku.JPG|Statue of Ninomiya Kinjiro File:161223 Hotoku-Ninomiya-jinja Odawara Japan05n.jpg|Hōtoku Hall File:161223 Hotoku-Ninomiya-jinja Odawara Japan08s3.jpg|Kinjiro Café </gallery>
== Access == * About a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Odawara Station|en|Odawara Station|qid=Q801290|lt=Odawara Station|1=Odawara Station}}. Located southwest of [[Odawara Castle Park]].
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Articles == * [[Ninomiya Shrine (disambiguation)]] * [[Shrine café]]
== External Links == *[http://www.ninomiya.or.jp Hōtoku Ninomiya Shrine] - Official website * {{Facebook}} *[https://www.hotoku.or.jp Hōtoku Museum]
{{Commonscat|Hōtoku-Ninomiya-jinja}} {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}}
==History== Ninomiya Sontoku was born in the village Kayama (栢山) in Odawara. And as for the great agricultural work he did in his lifetime and his moral and ethical teachings, the Hōtoku Association founded the Shrine in April 1894 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji|1=Meiji era}} 27) on the Odawara Castle ground.<ref>{{Cite web |title=Hotoku Museum |url=http://www.hotoku.or.jp |access-date=2018-01-29 |website=www.hotoku.or.jp}}</ref>
==Buildings== On the Shrine grounds are the main hall called "Honden" in which the spirituality is enshrined. Right next to the main building is the sub hall called "Gokitouden" which is used for ceremonies, if the main hall is already occupied. Between the second and third Shrine gate called Torii, are two local coffee shops ( Cafe Kinjiro, Cafe Kankitsu Club), where the staff sell original goods of the enshrined spirituality and local food. On the opposite site of the coffee shop is the "Hotoku Kaikan" a Wedding and Event hall in which all kinds of different projects are made: For example, a project to support local farmers "Kankitsu Club", an inbound tourism project called "Local Japan" and much more.
source: http://www.hotoku.jp/
==See also== * [[List of Shinto shrines in Japan]]
==References== {{Reflist}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}}
